title: pelargonium × ampliatum
intro: Pelargonium × ampliatum, also known as the Cape Geranium or Red Tip Geranium, is a hybrid plant species within the Geraniaceae family, resulting from the cross between Pelargonium betulina and Pelargonium capitatum. The leaves and stems of Pelargonium × ampliatum contain compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to make medicinal preparations. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of digestive issues, fever reduction, and immune system support. Herbal preparations of Pelargonium × ampliatum can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of certain health conditions.
